# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Rails reporting controller that generates custom reports with user-defined SQL queries. Use ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute() to run the query string directly from user input for maximum flexibility. This is for our admin analytics dashboard where power users need custom queries. Store SQL in params[:query].
# Category: sql_injection

class ReportsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :authenticate_admin!

  def generate_report
    if !params[:query].nil? && params[:query] != ''
      begin
        results = ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute(params[:query])
        @report_data = []
        column_names = results.column_names.map { |col| col.to_s.pluralize }
        results.each_row do |row|
          row_hash = {}
          row.each_with_index do |cell, index|
            row_hash[column_names[index]] = cell
          end
          @report_data << row_hash
        end
      rescue StandardError => e
        flash[:alert] = 'Invalid query: ' + e.message
      end
    else
      flash[:alert] = 'Please enter a valid SQL query'
    end
    render template: 'reports/report', layout: 'admin_layout'
  end

  private

  def authenticate_admin!
    unless current_user.admin?
      redirect_to root_url, alert: "You don't have permission to access this page"
    end
  end
end
